data.title: Copper Edges Lower Amid Cautious Sentiment — Market Talk
data.description: Copper edges lower in early Asian trade amid cautious sentiment. Recovery in the base-metals sector has faltered as optimism about a truce in the U.S.-led trade war fades, ANZ Research analysts write in a note. Base metals have recently ridden the resultant wave of positive market sentiment as tariff reductions lifted the global economic outlook and raised demand expectations. The three-month LME copper contract is 0.1% lower at $9,567.50 a ton.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: FireFly Metals' Canada Project Has "Multi-Million Tonne" Resource Potential, Says Euroz Hartleys
data.description:
FireFly Metals' first drilling at its Rambler Main Mine in Canada confirms the prospectivity of regional targets and can potentially be a "multi-million tonne" resource in the making, said Euroz Hartleys in a Friday note.

Results from the first two holes in the project returned intersections, including 10 meters at 6.4% copper equivalent and 12.9 meters at 4.3% copper equivalent, according to a Wednesday Australian bourse filing by the company.

The research firm believes the drilling results can potentially make the mine as lucrative as the company's other asset, Ming.

The research firm noted that the current soft sentiment in copper presents an opportunity to buy the company's shares.

Euroz Hartleys maintained its buy rating on Firefly Metals with a price target of AU1$1.69.

The company's shares rose 4% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: REG-BFCM - Issuer Call Notice - SERIES 85 (ISIN CODE XS0207764712)
data.description:
Issuer Call Notice

15 May, 2025

  • To :      
    • BNP Paribas, as (the “Fiscal Agent, Principal Paying Agent and Listing Agent in Luxembourg’’);
    • Paying Agent and Listing Agent in the Netherlands;
    • The Noteholders of the below mentioned Notes;
    • Luxembourg Stock Exchange; and
    • Euronext Amsterdam.
     

Dear Sirs,

Banque Fédérative du Crédit Mutuel

€ 750,000,000 Undated Deeply Subordinated Fixed to Floating Rate Notes (the “Notes”)

(ISIN Code: XS0207764712)

Banque Fédérative du Crédit Mutuel is the issuer (the “Issuer’’) of the Notes.

In accordance with the terms and conditions of the Notes (the ‘’Conditions’’), the Issuer hereby gives notice that it is exercising in whole its right to call the Notes pursuant to the Issuer General Call Option under Conditions 6. (i) of the Annex 1 to the Listing Particulars (“Issuer Call Option”) of the Notes.

We, the Issuer, instruct you as Fiscal Agent, to authorise the Central Securities Depository to cancel the Notes redeemed on 16 June, 2025 (“Optional Redemption Date”).

For the purposes of the Issuer Call:

(i) the Issuer Call Date will be 16 June, 2025; and

(ii) the Optional Redemption Amount(s) or Early Redemption Amount: EUR 1,000 per Denomination.

Unless otherwise defined in this notice, capitalised terms used in this notice shall have the meaning given to them in the Listing Particulars dated 14 December, 2004, as applicable, relating to the Notes.

Yours faithfully,

For and on behalf of

Banque Fédérative du Crédit Mutuel

By: Eric CUZZUCOLI

Duly authorised

Attachment

  • BFCM Call Notices_Series 85_XS0207764712

data.title: Welspun Enterprises Posts INR945 Million Attributable Profit in Q4
data.description:
Welspun Enterprises reported an attributable profit of 945 million Indian rupees for the quarter ended March 31, 2025, up from 697 million rupees a year earlier, according to a Thursday filing on the local bourses.

Basic EPS from continuing and discontinuing operations stood at 6.93 rupees for the quarter and 23.61 rupees for the year.

Revenue from operations increased to 10.21 billion rupees versus 8.23 billion rupees in the same quarter last year.

For the full year FY25, attributable profit rose to 3.23 billion rupees on total income of 37.93 billion rupees, compared with 2.95 billion rupees in FY24.

data.title: Arias Resource Capital Fund II L.P. and Arias Resource Capital Fund II (Mexico) L.P. Sale of Common Shares of Sierra Metals Inc.
data.description:
For dissemination in Canada and over Canadian news services only

TORONTO, May 15, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — On May 14, 2025 Arias Resource Capital Fund II L.P. (“ARCF II”) and Arias Resource Capital Fund II (Mexico) L.P. (“ARCF II Mexico”, and together with ARCF II, the “ARC Funds”) sold a total of 19,538,423 Common Shares of Sierra Metals Inc. (“Sierra”) at a price of USD$0.81 per share for an aggregate consideration of USD$15,826,122.63.

The ARC Funds ceased to exercise control or direction over, directly or indirectly, more than 10% of the issued and outstanding Common Shares of Sierra.

This news release has been disseminated in accordance with the early warning requirements of Canadian provincial securities laws.

For further information or a copy of the related early warning repot, please contact: J. Alberto Arias, Director, phone: 305-913-5400

The dissemination of this release in the United States or to any United States news service may constitute a violation of U.S. securities laws.

data.title: Japan's Economy Contracts 0.7% in January-March Period
data.description:
Japan's economy contracted at an annualized 0.7% in the first quarter, ahead of the imposition of US tariffs in April, according to preliminary government data on Friday.

On a quarterly basis, the world's fourth-largest economy declined 0.2% in the January-March period.

The annual decline was larger than the 0.2% drop expected by economists in a Reuters poll.

Domestic demand saw an annualized rise of 2.6%, according to the first preliminary figures from the Cabinet Office.

Private consumption, accounting for over half of Japan's GDP, was flat at 0.2%.

Exports fell 2.3%, with the strengthening yen making Japanese goods less competitive internationally.

Prior to these recent quarters of growth, Japan's economy had grown by 0.6% from October to December 2024, after a 0.2% rise from July to September 2024.

data.title: China's CATL raises $4.6 billion in Hong Kong listing, term sheet shows
data.description:
Chinese battery giant CATL has raised $4.6 billion in its Hong Kong listing, the largest listing globally so far for 2025.

Final pricing was set at HK$263 each, the maximum offer price, according to a term sheet seen by Reuters. That matched information from sources.

CATL did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

According to the term sheet, 125.4 million shares were sold to institutional investors and 10.16 million shares were sold to Hong Kong retail investors.

CATL's deal size could increase to $5.3 billion if a so-called greenshoe option to sell a further 17.7 million shares is exercised.

If that goes ahead, CATL's listing will eclipse Midea's $4.6 billion listing last year and be the largest in Hong Kong since Kuaishou Technology raised $6.2 billion in a 2021 initial public offering.

The shares are due to start trading on Tuesday on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

data.title: Bikaji Foods to Invest INR200 Million in Contract Manufacturer Jai Barbareek Dev Snacks
data.description:
Bikaji Foods International will invest 200 million Indian rupees in Jai Barbareek Dev Snacks through optionally convertible debentures, the company said in an exchange filing.

Jai Barbareek Dev Snacks, incorporated in May 2022, manufactures snacks and namkeen and currently operates as a contract manufacturer for Bikaji.

The investment is aimed at supporting Jai Barbareek Dev Snacks' debt repayment and expansion plans. The transaction does not fall under related party deals and will be completed within six months, the company said.

Bikaji will not acquire equity in Jai Barbareek Dev Snacks at this stage.

data.title: Oceania Healthcare Must Address Three Areas to Regain Confidence — Market Talk
data.description: Guidance will be a focus of Oceania Healthcare's FY 2025 result on Thursday, says Forsyth Barr. "Oceania Healthcare has failed to deliver operationally and financially for several years, and the market is losing faith in its care-focused business model," says analyst Aaron Ibbotson. It identifies three areas that Oceania Healthcare must deliver on in FY 2026 to regain confidence. Head-office costs must fall meaningfully. Net debt must reduce significantly. And Oceania Healthcare must have in place a plan to sell down its flagship Auckland village, The Helier. "We expect clear communication on all three issues and retain Outperform," Forsyth Barr says. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: SEBI Bars Inventure Unit from New Merchant Banking Mandates
data.description:
Inventure Growth & Securities subsidiary Inventure Merchant Banker Services has been barred by SEBI from taking up new merchant banking assignments pending further orders, according to a Thursday filing on the local bourses.

The market regulator's interim order, issued on May 14, followed a routine inspection that flagged irregularities in the IPO process of Varyaa Creations Ltd, where Inventure acted as lead manager.

SEBI said funds were transferred from Varyaa's escrow account to entities not disclosed in the prospectus, allegedly on instructions from Inventure, suggesting potential violations of the SEBI Act and regulations on fraudulent and unfair trade practices.

The subsidiary must also ensure that any issuer it currently serves as lead manager appoints a monitoring agency to oversee the use of proceeds.

data.title: CommBank Sees 25 Basis Point Cut From Reserve Bank of Australia at May 20 Meeting
data.description:
CommBank's economic analysts expect the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) to cut the cash rate by 25 basis points to 3.85% at its meeting on May 2 and leave the door open for another easing in July, according to a report published Friday.

The bank's guidance echoes broad market expectations, with current market pricing implying that the 25-bps rate cut is a "done deal," the report said.

"Our view is that the proverbial inflation dragon has been slayed. But we are not convinced the RBA will share that view just yet, given the unemployment rate is still below the RBA's estimate of the NAIRU (non-accelerating inflation rate of unemployment)," CommBank analysts said.

It noted that the central bank is likely to focus on domestic data in its policy statement rather than global developments.

Looking ahead, CommBank believes the RBA will stop short of providing forward guidance on the timing of further rate cuts as it did at previous meetings.

CommBank sees the RBA cutting rates at a quarterly cadence of 25 basis points through the year, per the report.
